title: Cyclic Sighing OS
thesis: Five minutes a day. The strongest single-modality result in any 2023 anxiety-reduction trial — including mindfulness meditation. Free, free of side effects, free of equipment.
meta_description: The five-minute breathing protocol with the strongest evidence in any 2023 RCT. The mechanism, the steps, and a wallet card.
og_description: Five minutes a day. The strongest single-modality result in any 2023 anxiety-reduction trial.
twitter_description: Five minutes a day. Strongest evidence in any 2023 anxiety-reduction trial.
schema_keywords: cyclic sighing, physiological sigh, Balban Stanford 2023, anxiety reduction, breathing protocol, parasympathetic

pull_quote.text: Five minutes daily of cyclic sighing produced the highest improvement in positive affect (1.91 vs. 1.22 for mindfulness, p < 0.05) and the greatest reduction in respiratory rate over 28 days.
pull_quote.cite: — Balban et al. (2023), Cell Reports Medicine — Stanford

steps.name: Inhale through the nose, expanding low
steps.body: Breathe in slowly through your nose.

steps.name: Top off — a second small sip
steps.body: At the top of the inhale, take a second small sip of air through the nose.

steps.name: Repeat for five minutes — then settle
steps.body: Continue the cycle for five minutes.

items.text: Sit or lie comfortably. One hand on chest, one on belly.
items.help: Belly should rise more than chest.

items.text: Slow breath in. Expand the lower ribs and belly.
items.help: Not the upper chest. Send the breath low.

items.text: At the top of the inhale, take a second short sip through the nose.
items.help: This is the signature move. Reinflates the alveoli.

items.text: Twice as long as the inhale. Let it pour out.
items.help: The longer the exhale, the more parasympathetic.

items.text: No counting. Follow the rhythm. Sit quietly for one minute after.
items.help: Daily practice is where the 28-day shift comes from.
